target.name: StoreDot
target.founder: Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, Gil Rosenman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot, a Series E climate and energy company, has raised $206.5M to date. The valuation landscape in Climate & Energy includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers spanning adjacent stages and valuation tiers, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The stage-progression method is applicable and yields a valuation of $289M, derived by applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E progression to the $206M total-raised proxy. The peer-median approach is not applicable: while 21 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) returned fewer than three datapoints, making a sector-wide median misleading rather than instructive.

Given the single applicable method, the analysis supports a range of $246M (low) to $332M (high), with a median of $289M. The range reflects the inherent uncertainty when triangulation relies on one method rather than multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for this specific company and stage (1.4x assumes typical progression but StoreDot's battery technology profile may warrant adjustment), and the absence of tightly comparable peers at Series E in the sector, which limits ability to validate against market comps.
